stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains, including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama-3 indicates relevance to the current state-of-the-art in large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML concepts like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The source, Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicates this is a community/model spotlight rather than a breakthrough announcement.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents struggle with verifiable source attribution, despite synthesizing information from numerous web sources.
Current methods (self-citation or standard RAG) fail to reliably validate source accessibility, relevance, or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel evaluation framework using an AST parser to address the critical issue of citation verification in LLM agents.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, optimizing AI workloads for the ecosystem.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device, emphasizing privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
stories.author: Justine Calma
bullet_points: Massive data centers are the physical infrastructure supporting the expansion of AI technologies.
The rapid build-out of these energy-intensive facilities is causing global conflict.
Key concerns surrounding AI data centers include strain on power grids, environmental impact, and community opposition.

stories.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly re-engaging with Intel to use Intel-made chips for its hardware.
This marks a potential reversal or parallel path after Apple's successful transition to its proprietary Apple Silicon.
The agreement is described as 'preliminary' according to reporting from The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The method is built upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models conditioned on scene depth.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI Co-Mathematician' workbench designed to assist mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system leverages AI agents to provide holistic support across the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace capable of managing uncertainty and tracking failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a novel scene while allowing per-frame control over camera intrinsic and extrinsic parameters.
The model leverages any pre-trained image-to-video diffusion model, conditioning it using scene depth and other inputs.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The paper challenges the reliability of current global LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the best-fit Bradley-Terry ranking is misleading.
The study concludes that the top 50 ranked models are statistically indistinguishable, with win probabilities barely exceeding 0.53.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current Retrieval-Augmented Agents (RAG) which treat retrieval as a 'black box'.
Existing RAG methods mimic novice searchers by iteratively refining queries based on retrieved snippets.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to incorporate expert knowledge (strong priors) to improve efficiency and reduce latency.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to standard modern NLP infrastructure.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is introducing experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code in a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: PlayStation sees AI as a ‘powerful tool’ to help make games
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Sony (PlayStation) views AI as a 'powerful tool' in game development.
The company detailed its evaluation of AI integration during an earnings presentation.
The adoption of generative AI in AAA games is noted, contrasting with resistance from some indie developers.

stories.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Elon Musk is facing a criminal probe in France.
The probe relates to an unspecified 'X case'.
France is threatening criminal charges if Musk fails to appear for questioning.

stories.title: Chrome's 4GB AI model isn't new, but you're not wrong for being confused
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Chrome is utilizing a local 4GB AI model.
The article addresses user confusion regarding this large local AI footprint.
Users have the option to disable Chrome's local AI storage.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: UniPool proposes a new approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures, challenging the traditional rigid per-layer expert allocation.
The research suggests that the assumption of isolated expert capacity per layer might be overly restrictive, as demonstrated by routing probe results.
The study indicates that replacing a layer's learned router with random routing causes only minor accuracy degradation (1.0-1.6 points), suggesting flexibility in expert sharing.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key limitation in LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems for training.
It introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification mechanisms to ensure the generated problems are mathematically sound.
This work aims to move beyond reliance on expensive human experts or flawed self-play methods for problem generation in AI research.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in the pretraining and finetuning of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The study observes that using the exact same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during the Supervised Finetuning (SFT) stage.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployment validation.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user's search behavior.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond black-box retrieval by incorporating expert-level priors to improve efficiency and reduce latency.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the ambiguity in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) progress, questioning if reported gains are genuine or due to inconsistent evaluation.
Current MMDG research is fragmented, showing significant variation across datasets, modality setups, and experimental procedures.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for being too narrow, focusing heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework that integrates explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making sequences.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional design where each transformer layer has a separate, isolated expert set.
Suggests that uniform random routing can maintain high accuracy, implying that rigid per-layer expert allocation might be overly restrictive.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure the generated mathematical problems are sound.
Offers an alternative to expensive human expert involvement or flawed self-play methods in problem generation.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The paper investigates the role of optimizers in Large Language Model (LLM) training, specifically during finetuning.
A key finding is that using the same optimizer from pretraining during full finetuning results in a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This approach reportedly outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during Supervised Finetuning (SFT).

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific domain.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are heavily skewed towards action recognition, overlooking other critical real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) by exploring Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
A key problem identified is that restricting MoE inference to a subset of experts for specific tasks causes significant performance degradation.
The research proposes 'EMO' (Pretraining Mixture of Experts) as a method to enhance the practical utility and modularity of MoEs.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems.
The proposed method, VHG, introduces a verifier-backed approach to ensure the generated problems are mathematically sound, overcoming issues with naive self-play.
This work is relevant to advancing LLM training and enabling more autonomous scientific research by providing a robust problem generation mechanism.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established benchmark or ground-truth labels exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, which are often too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategy into RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment during extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new reinforcement learning approach.
RAO is designed to train recursive agents capable of spawning and delegating sub-tasks to themselves.
This recursive structure enables agents to scale to longer contexts and tackle complex problems using a divide-and-conquer strategy.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current LLM reasoning research: the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying training scaling.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical axes of difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to systematically improve long-horizon reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the non-trivial problem of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Dirty Frag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: openwall.com via lattera
bullet_points: The article discusses a 'Dirty Frag,' which is a type of Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.
The vulnerability is described as 'Universal,' suggesting it may affect a broad range of Linux systems.
This falls under cybersecurity news, indicating a potential security risk for Linux users and administrators.
